2606.10675.title: Multilingual Word-Level Forced Alignment with Self-Supervised Representations and Learned Dynamic Programming
2606.10675.paper_summary: We present a method for accurate multilingual word-level forced alignment, consisting of an alignment encoder and a learned alignment decoder. The encoder integrates two representations: one from the Massively Multilingual Speech (MMS) model and another from a self-supervised phoneme boundary detector (UnSupSeg). It learns to fuse them and to estimate word-boundary probabilities over long temporal contexts. The alignment decoder is a learned dynamic programming that combines encoder outputs with segmental features over the MMS and UnSupSeg representations to infer final word boundaries. Trained iteratively on TIMIT and Buckeye, the proposed approach outperforms Montreal Forced Aligner (MFA) and MMS-based alignment on both datasets. On unseen languages (Dutch, German, and Hebrew), the proposed model achieves performance consistently better than or on par with existing alignment approaches, indicating its potential to scale to 1100+ languages supported by MMS without further training.
2606.10675.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一種準確的多語言單詞級強制對齊方法，該方法由對齊編碼器和學習的對齊解碼器組成。編碼器整合了兩種表示：一種來自大規模多語言語音（MMS）模型，另一種來自自我監督的音素邊界檢測器（UnSupSeg）。它學會融合這兩者並在長時間上下文中估計單詞邊界概率。對齊解碼器是一種學習的動態規劃，將編碼器輸出與MMS和UnSupSeg表示的段特徵結合，以推斷最終的單詞邊界。該方法在TIMIT和Buckeye上進行了迭代訓練，並在這兩個數據集上超越了蒙特利爾強制對齊器（MFA）和基於MMS的對齊。在未見過的語言（荷蘭語、德語和希伯來語）上，該模型的表現始終優於或與現有的對齊方法相當，顯示出其在不進一步訓練的情況下，能夠擴展到MMS支持的1100多種語言的潛力。
2606.10675.author: Roy Weber et.al.
2606.10675.authors: Roy Weber, Meidan Zehavi, Rotem Rousso, Joseph Keshet
